display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: New automation on an object that already has automation

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: How many record-triggered automations already on this DML event?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Greenfield — apply the Flow-vs-Apex tree normally

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does the new logic share data or ordering with the existing automation?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Consolidate INTO the existing entry point — add a branch/subflow or a handler method, do NOT add a parallel automation

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Is the existing entry point Apex, or declarative?

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Add a handler method in the existing one-trigger-per-object framework — ordered, recursion-guarded, testable

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Does the new concern need code-only capability? complex bulk / recursion control / mid-tx callout / unit tests

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Promote to Apex as the single entry point, call the Flow as a subflow or invocable — keep ONE entry point

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Add to the existing Flow as a branch, OR a second Flow with an explicit Flow Trigger Order value — never rely on unspecified order

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Re-confirm the recursion guard covers the new DML path

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p: Set explicit order; document the density decision
